learning_outcomes.description: Identify and describe theories and challenges of destination management and marketing.
Apply specific marketing theories and concepts to destination management and marketing.
Present recommendations that will improve destination management and marketing for associated stakeholders.
Critically analyse and evaluate the management and marketing of destinations.
Design an IMC digital market plan addressing a current tourism issue pertinent to DMOs.
Work as a team to conduct a market analysis of a destination.

assessments.task_description:
Overview

You are a team of external tourism marketing consultants who have been engaged by a Destination Marketing Organisation (DMO) to conduct a case study analysis of a destination* of your choice.

 A Case Study analysis is an important technique that DMOs rely on. It allows DMOs to gain a comprehensive understanding of a destination, which is a critical basis for them to be able to develop effective tourism marketing strategies.

Your case study analysis must include:

·       a description of your destination using Weaver’s elements of a destination;

·       an explanation of how your destination is currently managed;

·       an evaluation of the competitiveness of your destination (using the destination competitiveness model);

·       an analysis of how your destination is currently marketed;

·       an analysis of how your destination currently employs product development strategies;

·       identification of primary and secondary stakeholders;

·       recommendations.

4000 words

*Destination – Choose your own destination. This must be a specific city (e.g. Melbourne, Shanghai) or region (e.g. Fraser Coast, Tropical North Queensland) or state/county (e.g. Queensland, Yorkshire, California) or country (e.g. New Zealand, Bangladesh). Do not choose an amusement park or a specific activity (e.g. tour). Confirm your destination with your course co-ordinator by Week 3.

A detailed explanation of the assessment requirements and the marking rubric will be discussed in class and made available on the course Blackboard.

Peer evaluation will be used to moderate each team member's mark.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assignment is to be submitted through Turnitin on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You are an independent tourism marketing consultant. A Destination Marketing Organisation (DMO) has engaged you to develop an Integrated Marketing Communications (IMC) Plan in response to a current tourism issue. The time period for your IMC Plan is from November 30, 2026 to February 28, 2027.

It is strongly suggested that you use the DMO for your destination from Assessment 1. This will provide you with background information about your destination.

You will choose your own current tourism issue. This must be different to the issue chosen by other members of your team from Assessment 1. You must have your current tourism issue and DMO approved by your teaching team by Week 10.

Your IMC Plan is to be presented to the DMO as 6-page infographic. It must include:

·       A description of the destination, including its current promotional strategies.

·       Explanation of your current issue and your target market

·       Your IMC promotional objectives

·       Your IMC message strategy

·       Your IMC tactics

·       Analysis of emerging trends

The assignment is to be presented as an infographic (using Canva's Infographic function or similar). The infographic needs to be very specific and focused, so take time to complete all sections of the assignment. It should include pictures/graphs and not blocks of text. 

A detailed explanation of the assessment requirements and the marking rubric will be discussed in class and made available on the course Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Exam week 1 - Exam week 2

Your IMC discussion will be held during the end of semester exam block. Time and date of your IMC discussion will be advised in class and on Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
The DMO has reviewed your IMC Plan and has requested to meet with you to further discuss elements of your infographic.

In the examination period, you will be engaging in a 10-minute discussion on your IMC Plan and how it addresses your chosen tourism issue for your chosen destination.

The teaching team will role play the role of the DMO and will be asking the questions. You will be expected to answer these questions to pass this assessment piece.

The discussion will centre around the DMO asking you questions to gain further insights into the reasoning for, and implications of, various elements of your IMC Plan.

More information relating to the presentation will be presented in class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination Management & Marketing Intro

In this week students will understand the elements of destination management and marketing. How a destinations and Destination Marketing Organisations (DMO)s are defined is also explained, as is the roles that a DMO performs at a destination. This knowledge will help students understand the major destination marketing and management roles that a DMO is required to perform.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination Management & Marketing Intro In this week students will understand the elements of destination management and marketing. How a destinations and Destination Marketing Organisations (DMO)s are defined is also explained, as is the roles that a DMO performs at a destination. This knowledge will help students understand the major destination marketing and management roles that a DMO is required to perform.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination lifecycles and planning issues

This week students will use the Tourism Area Lifecycle model and Crouch & Ritchie's (1999) destination competitiveness model to evaluate both the stage and competitiveness of a destination. Students will also apply the World Centre of Excellence's 10As of successful destinations. These models will help students to gain the knowledge that DMOs require to be able to effectively plan for destination management/marketing.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination lifecycles and planning issues This week students will use the Tourism Area Lifecycle model and Crouch & Ritchie's (1999) destination competitiveness model to evaluate both the stage and competitiveness of a destination. Students will also apply the World Centre of Excellence's 10As of successful destinations. These models will help students to gain the knowledge that DMOs require to be able to effectively plan for destination management/marketing.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination Marketing Planning

This week students will be introduced to the destination planning elements essential for DMOs including writing goals, objectives and mission statements. An introduction to destination marketing planning tactics is also provided to help students understand how planning elements are fulfilled.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination Marketing Planning This week students will be introduced to the destination planning elements essential for DMOs including writing goals, objectives and mission statements. An introduction to destination marketing planning tactics is also provided to help students understand how planning elements are fulfilled.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination Product Development

This week students will learn how the destination product is defined by a DMO when marketing and managing a destination to tourists and stakeholders. DMO product development roles are also described as are the product development variations by types of destinations. This information is important as it helps students understand what constitutes a successful product development process and how this can be controlled.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination Product Development This week students will learn how the destination product is defined by a DMO when marketing and managing a destination to tourists and stakeholders. DMO product development roles are also described as are the product development variations by types of destinations. This information is important as it helps students understand what constitutes a successful product development process and how this can be controlled.

learning_activities.topic:
Governance & Stakeholder Management

This week introduces students to the importance of partnership and stakeholder management by communicating to students that DMOs need to work with other organisations to successfully manage and market a destination. Barriers and challenges for destination partnerships are provided, and theories such as stakeholder theory and governance are explored as tools to help DMOs manage a destination.
learning_activities.topic_description: Governance & Stakeholder Management This week introduces students to the importance of partnership and stakeholder management by communicating to students that DMOs need to work with other organisations to successfully manage and market a destination. Barriers and challenges for destination partnerships are provided, and theories such as stakeholder theory and governance are explored as tools to help DMOs manage a destination.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination Markets

This week focuses on how DMOs can efficiently and effectively segment tourists into groups based on criteria that is appropriate for DMO marketing and management purposes. Popular market segmentation criteria such as tourists' motivations to travel and geographic profiling are evaluated. Students will also learn the financial and resource challenges DMO's are faced with when segmenting tourists into preferred groups.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination Markets This week focuses on how DMOs can efficiently and effectively segment tourists into groups based on criteria that is appropriate for DMO marketing and management purposes. Popular market segmentation criteria such as tourists' motivations to travel and geographic profiling are evaluated. Students will also learn the financial and resource challenges DMO's are faced with when segmenting tourists into preferred groups.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination Markets, Target Marketing & Destination Image

This week students understand how DMOs employ target marketing criteria to focus on tourists (segments) that will most likely provide the DMOs with a return on investment. Students will also learn to understand the principles of destination image in that DMOs must promote attributes that will attract a tourist segment to the DMO' s destination.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination Markets, Target Marketing & Destination Image This week students understand how DMOs employ target marketing criteria to focus on tourists (segments) that will most likely provide the DMOs with a return on investment. Students will also learn to understand the principles of destination image in that DMOs must promote attributes that will attract a tourist segment to the DMO' s destination.

learning_activities.topic:
Crisis Recovery & Visitor Management

This week students will examine how major crises and disasters affect destinations and how DMOs plan and respond to these catastrophes. This week serves as the basis for understanding current internal and external issues that could impact the success of a DMO's management and marketing of a destination.
learning_activities.topic_description: Crisis Recovery & Visitor Management This week students will examine how major crises and disasters affect destinations and how DMOs plan and respond to these catastrophes. This week serves as the basis for understanding current internal and external issues that could impact the success of a DMO's management and marketing of a destination.

learning_activities.topic:
Destination Positioning & Branding

This week students will explore how preferred tourists (segments) can be attracted by DMOs through promoting a destination/s core values and identity. Students will also learn to evaluate the opportunities and challenges of destination positioning and destination branding during this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Destination Positioning & Branding This week students will explore how preferred tourists (segments) can be attracted by DMOs through promoting a destination/s core values and identity. Students will also learn to evaluate the opportunities and challenges of destination positioning and destination branding during this week.

learning_activities.topic:
No seminar - In-semester Break

This week students will have the opportunity to work independently on their Infographic. Here, it is expected that the tourism issue relevant to their chosen destination will be determined.
learning_activities.topic_description: No seminar - In-semester Break This week students will have the opportunity to work independently on their Infographic. Here, it is expected that the tourism issue relevant to their chosen destination will be determined.

learning_activities.topic:
IMC for Destinations

This week students are introduced to Integrated Marketing Communications theory and how this is applied by DMOs to market their destination. Benefits and challenges of IMC are learnt, and students will understand the 5 IMC stages that DMOs must perform to effectively plan and manage a successful IMC Campaign.
learning_activities.topic_description: IMC for Destinations This week students are introduced to Integrated Marketing Communications theory and how this is applied by DMOs to market their destination. Benefits and challenges of IMC are learnt, and students will understand the 5 IMC stages that DMOs must perform to effectively plan and manage a successful IMC Campaign.

learning_activities.topic:
Technology & Destination Management

This week students will be introduced to and understand how technologies advancements have revolutionised how DMOs are marketing and managing destinations through new forms of technology (e.g. digital media). Travellers' usage of digital media across all stages of their trip cycle are evaluated as is the usage of user generated, marketer generated and co-created content when marketing destinations.
learning_activities.topic_description: Technology & Destination Management This week students will be introduced to and understand how technologies advancements have revolutionised how DMOs are marketing and managing destinations through new forms of technology (e.g. digital media). Travellers' usage of digital media across all stages of their trip cycle are evaluated as is the usage of user generated, marketer generated and co-created content when marketing destinations.

learning_activities.topic:
ICTs, evaluation, emerging issues & discussion

This week students will learn about ICT developments such as artificial intelligence and virtual reality and how they are changing the way that a DMO is currently managing and marketing a destination. Other managing and marketing emerging issues such a focus on environmental management and restorative tourism are also discussed.
learning_activities.topic_description: ICTs, evaluation, emerging issues & discussion This week students will learn about ICT developments such as artificial intelligence and virtual reality and how they are changing the way that a DMO is currently managing and marketing a destination. Other managing and marketing emerging issues such a focus on environmental management and restorative tourism are also discussed.

learning_activities.topic:
Review and Consolidate

The final week provides students with the opportunity to prepare and discuss their IMC (Infographic) and oral interview with the Course Co-Ordinator. A final overview of destination management and marketing is also provided.
learning_activities.topic_description: Review and Consolidate The final week provides students with the opportunity to prepare and discuss their IMC (Infographic) and oral interview with the Course Co-Ordinator. A final overview of destination management and marketing is also provided.
